summary: Responsive overhaul verified end-to-end for Ghar Ka Zaiqa. Tested 10 viewport widths (320, 375, 414, 480, 640, 768, 1024, 1280, 1440, 1920). NO horizontal scroll at any width. Hero, navbar, drawer, auth modal, cart drawer, checkout, admin dashboard, and chef dashboard all render correctly at mobile/tablet/desktop. Customer register+cart flow, admin login+dashboard, and chef login+dashboard all functional.
flows_verified: Burger drawer opens with Menu/Offers/Reviews/About + Login button at <=880px
Auth modal centered, 335x525 in 375x812 viewport, all 5 register fields rendered
Customer registration via Create Account succeeds, user chip appears in navbar, logout in drawer
Add to cart from mobile menu, badge increments, FAB opens cart drawer at 100% viewport width (375px)
Checkout form (name, phone, area, address, place-order) renders fully visible at 375px
Admin stats: 2x2 grid at 375px, 1x4 from 768px upward
Admin tabs bar scrolls horizontally at 375px (sw=390 > w=375) without overflowing page
Chef dashboard: filters wrap on mobile (58px tall) vs single-row on larger (39-40px), no horizontal overflow at 375/768/1280
test_report_links: /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (from prior iteration, not re-run – frontend-only request)
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: One console warning observed during cart flow about an empty string being passed to an img/anchor attribute ('An empty string was passed to the %s attribute. This may cause the browser to download the whole page again'). Likely a `src=""` or `href=""` fallback on a conditionally-rendered element. Not blocking but should be fixed to avoid an extra request. Search for `src={...}` / `href={...}` where the variable can be empty and gate the element with `&&`.

Navbar burger remains visible at 768px and 1024px is the first width where nav-pill appears; spec said burger at <=880px which is satisfied, but consider lowering the breakpoint so 1024 actually swaps to nav-pill as described (currently observed: 1024 = nav-pill OK, 768 = burger OK).
Home.jsx and AdminDashboard.jsx remain close to the 700-line file guideline (carry-over from iteration 1); future refactor recommended.
Cart-drawer at 375px takes 100% width as required; visually good but a subtle dimmed overlay behind it on mobile would improve focus (optional polish).
test_credentials: Admin: admin@gharkazaiqa.com / GharKaZaiqa@2026 ; Chef: chef@gharkazaiqa.com / ChefZaiqa2026 ; Customer registered dynamically as resp-test-<ts>@example.com / Test@1234.
seed_data_creation: Registered one ephemeral test customer (resp-test-<timestamp>@example.com) and added one dish to cart. No order placed (WhatsApp redirect avoided as per request). Cart cleared by logout.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Responsive overhaul is verified across 320 → 1920px. If new layout work is done, re-run /app/test_reports script pattern from iteration_2 (viewport sweep + checks for horizontal_scroll, burger/nav_pill toggles, hero column count, admin_stats_grid). All data-testids documented in the review request are present and working. Public URL is https://109385fe-9171-429b-99b9-c6a8e326b161.preview.emergentagent.com. Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py is the regression source if backend changes occur.
rca of the issue: N/A — no regressions detected after responsive overhaul. All viewports render without horizontal scrollbars and all critical user flows (auth, cart, admin, chef) continue to work.
